title: Saint Lubin (Leobinus)
description: Bishop of Chartres, venerated in Catholic tradition. Origin of the name's usage in France as a masculine first name. The name derives from the Germanic baptismal name Leobin, meaning 'dear' or 'beloved'.
da_description: Biskop af Chartres, æret i den katolske tradition. Navnets brug i Frankrig som maskulint fornavn stammer herfra. Navnet er afledt af det germanske dåbsnavn Leobin, der betyder 'kær' eller 'elsket'.

title: "Frère Lubin" — Clément Marot
description: The French Renaissance poet Clément Marot wrote the satirical poem 'Frère Lubin', establishing Lubin as a recognizable literary archetype in French culture — a roguish, hypocritical monk. The name subsequently became a stock character name in French theater and literature.
da_description: Den franske renæssancedigter Clément Marot skrev det satiriske digt 'Frère Lubin', som etablerede Lubin som en genkendelig litterær arketyp i den franske kultur — en skurkagtig, hyklerisk munk. Efterfølgende blev navnet en standardfigurenavn i fransk teater og litteratur.

title: Lubin Baugin
description: French Baroque painter known for religious works and still lifes. His famous painting 'Nature morte à l'échiquier' (Still Life with Chessboard) is displayed at the Musée du Louvre. He was Painter to the King in 1657.
da_description: Fransk barokmaler kendt for religiøse værker og stillebliver. Hans berømte maleri 'Nature morte à l'échiquier' (Stillleben med skakbræt) er udstillet på Musée du Louvre. Han var Kongens Maler i 1657.

title: Molière — George Dandin
description: Molière's comedy George Dandin, premiered at Versailles on 18 July 1668, features a character named Lubin — a naive peasant servant who plays a central role in the plot. The play demonstrates the widespread use of Lubin as a French masculine name in 17th-century literature and theater.
da_description: Molières komedie George Dandin, uropført på Versailles den 18. juli 1668, indeholder en figur ved navn Lubin — en naiv bondekned-tjener, der spiller en central rolle i handlingen. Skuespillet demonstrerer den udbredte brug af Lubin som et fransk maskulint navn i det 17. århundredes litteratur og teater.

title: "Annette et Lubin"
description: Major literary work by Jean-François Marmontel featuring the name Lubin as the male protagonist. This pastoral tale was adapted into operas by multiple composers and performed throughout Europe.
da_description: Betydningsfuldt litterært værk af Jean-François Marmontel med Lubin som den mandlige protagonist. Denne pastorale fortælling blev tilpasset til operaer af flere komponister og opført i hele Europa.

title: "Au clair de la lune"
description: Traditional French folk song featuring a character named Lubin. The song has been sung throughout France for centuries and is taught in schools.
da_description: Traditionel fransk folkesang med en figur ved navn Lubin. Sangen er blevet sunget i hele Frankrig i århundreder og undervises i skolerne.

title: Lubin de Beauvais
description: French painter, watercolourist, and illustrator. He collaborated with major Parisian newspapers and magazines including Le Rire and La Vie Parisienne. His works are held in the Musée d'Orsay collection.
da_description: Fransk maler, akvarelmaler og illustrator. Han samarbejdede med store parisiske aviser og magasiner, herunder Le Rire og La Vie Parisienne. Hans værker er i Musée d'Orsay-samlingen.

title: 20th Century Usage
description: Between 1900 and 1999, INSEE records show at least 225 boys named Lubin in France (counts below 5 per year are not published by INSEE, so the true total may be slightly higher). The name saw modest, steady usage throughout the century.
da_description: Mellem 1900 og 1999 registrerer INSEE-data mindst 225 drenge ved navn Lubin i Frankrig (tal under 5 pr. år offentliggøres ikke af INSEE, så det sande total kan være lidt højere). Navnet havde beskeden, stabil brug gennem hele århundredet.

title: Contemporary Peak
description: 3,935 boys named Lubin in France during this period, with a peak of 245 births in 2012. As of 2024, Lubin ranked 620th among boys' names with 70 births.
da_description: 3.935 drenge fik navnet Lubin i Frankrig i denne periode med et toppunkt på 245 fødsler i 2012. I 2024 rangerede Lubin nr. 620 blandt drengenavne med 70 fødsler.
